title: Part 2: Token IDs Become Hidden States

cues.label: Map
cues.headline: You are here in the LLM pipeline
cues.narration: Text has already been tokenized. This section explains the handoff into model-owned numeric state.

cues.label: Token IDs
cues.headline: Token IDs are addresses, not meaning
cues.narration: The list [12, 44, 91, 44] tells the model which rows to fetch from its learned embedding table.

cues.label: Embedding lookup
cues.headline: Each ID copies one learned vector row
cues.narration: ID 44 points to the same embedding row every time it appears, because the tokenizer and embedding table were trained as a matched pair.

cues.label: Position
cues.headline: Position vectors tell identical IDs where they are
cues.narration: The two 44 tokens start from the same learned row, then receive different position information because they appear in different slots.

cues.label: Hidden state
cues.headline: Rows stack into the first hidden-state matrix
cues.narration: The transformer does not consume raw text. It consumes this table: one row per token position and one column per hidden feature.

cues.label: Transformer
cues.headline: Transformer blocks edit the matrix next
cues.narration: This hidden-state matrix is the object attention and MLP layers will refine before the output head scores next-token logits.
